title: 1 Thessalonians: Faithful Awaiting
objective: Encourage holy endurance and hope in Christ's return amid affliction.

required_passages.text: Your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope... you turned to God from idols... to wait for his Son from heaven.

segments.audio_script: Paul affirms a young church persevering through suffering with vibrant hope.

Listen to 1 Thessalonians 1-3. Paul thanks God for their faith, love, and hope. They turned from idols to serve the living God and await Jesus from heaven. Despite persecution, they became examples to other believers. Paul expresses pastoral longing and sends Timothy to strengthen them.
Thessalonian believers faced hostility soon after conversion and needed reassurance.
Conversion includes turning and waiting: renouncing idols and expecting Christ. Healthy churches are formed by gospel reception, imitation, and multiplication through suffering.
Key themes include conversion, discipleship, perseverance, pastoral care, and eschatological hope.

Review question: What did Paul commend the Thessalonians for in 1 Thessalonians 1? Answer: their work of faith, labor of love, and steadfastness of hope in the Lord Jesus Christ — and their example that spread throughout Macedonia and Achaia.
segments.correct_answer: work of faith, labor of love, steadfastness of hope — and their example spread to all Macedonia and Achaia
acceptable_variants: the three virtues — faith hope love expressed in work labor and steadfastness
their faith became known throughout the region without paul needing to say anything
1 thessalonians 1 — work of faith labor of love hope of the lord, and a widespread testimony

segments.audio_script: Hope in Christ's return strengthens endurance. Next, Paul explains holiness and the resurrection hope of believers.

vocabulary.definition: Paul's affirmation in 1 Thessalonians 1:4 that the Thessalonians are beloved and chosen by God; the gospel came to them in power and the Spirit confirming their election
The summary of Thessalonian conversion in 1 Thessalonians 1:9 — they turned from idols to serve the living and true God and to wait for his Son from heaven; three elements of their conversion
